checks.a_analysis: Comparison of all content in the output JSON against the source PDF shows that every header, question, and paragraph corresponds directly to original text. No hallucinated or invented content was found.
The source document does not contain any contact information (phone, email, URLs, or addresses). The output faithfully reflects this and contains no fabricated contact data.
All 4 pages and 7 distinct questions/sections from the source document are fully present in the output. No pages or sections were omitted during conversion.
The 'Most Important EVP Attributes' table in the source has 10 data rows per group. The JSON output includes all 10 rows with their corresponding data values intact.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: A thorough review confirms that no substantial portions of text are missing within any section. The output captures the full depth of each response provided in the original document.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The output is a highly accurate transcription. It preserves specific formatting like underlining ('we', 'they') and captures the text exactly as written, including original typos found in the source. Minor normalization of hyphenation in 'one-size-fits-all' is considered acceptable reformatting.

checks.a_analysis: I have reviewed the entire JSON output and compared it with the source PDF. There are no instances of duplicated paragraphs, sections, or table content. The headers ('DRAFT 5-17-2010') and page numbers appearing on each page of the source were correctly identified as recurring document artifacts and excluded from the main content to avoid duplication.

The heading hierarchy is consistent. The document title is established as the main section title, and each of the seven questions from the source PDF is correctly identified as a Level 2 heading ('heading-1' through 'heading-7'). There are no skipped levels or incorrect nestings.

The table on page 3, 'Most Important EVP Attributes', is correctly structured. The JSON accurately captures the two-tiered header (the main title spanning both columns and the age group headers). The data rows are correctly aligned. In the fifth data row, the source PDF lists two items ('Retirement Benefits' and 'Respect') in the right column corresponding to a single item on the left; the JSON correctly merged these into one cell to maintain row alignment, preserving the table's logical structure.

The reading order of the JSON output follows the linear flow of the source PDF exactly, progressing from page 1 through page 4 in a logical sequence. Section headers and paragraphs are positioned correctly relative to one another.

The source document does not contain standard bulleted or numbered lists. The list-like data within the table on page 3 is correctly handled within the table structure rather than being forced into a list format.
The source document contains no hyperlinks or URLs. No links were injected or fabricated in the output.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output faithfully reproduces the single-column visual organization of the source PDF. The introduction of a Table of Contents (TOC) sidebar is an expected accessibility feature and does not disrupt the document's flow. While text wraps around this sidebar (e.g., splitting the paragraph 'Consequently, the State must manage talent...' into two visual blocks), the logical sequence of information remains intact and matches the source's intent. Information is located in the same relative positions.

The table 'Most Important EVP Attributes' from page 3 of the source PDF is correctly rendered in the HTML. It includes clearly defined borders, distinct headers, and maintains the two-column structure for different age groups. The cell content is accurate and legible, including more complex entries like the tied values in the 'Age Group: 50-59' column.
The source PDF is a text-based document without any meaningful images, logos, or charts. No images are expected in the HTML output, and none are missing.

There is no visual duplication of body content. While the section headings appear in the Table of Contents and again in the document body, this is an intentional and standard design for accessible navigation. No paragraphs, table rows, or other body elements are repeated incorrectly.
